title: Englerina parviflora
intro: Englerina parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, commonly known as the buckthorn family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Englerina parviflora include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Englerina parviflora is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and extracts, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
